mission.title: Install and Configure Cluster API (CAPI)
mission.description: Cluster API (CAPI) is a Kubernetes project that provides declarative APIs and tooling to simplify the provisioning, upgrading, and management of multiple Kubernetes clusters. It is particularly useful for platform operators looking to automate cluster lifecycle management across various infrastructure environments. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with Kubernetes concepts and potential complexity in multi-cluster management.

steps.title: Add the Cluster API Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Cluster API Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add cluster-api https://charts.cluster-api.sigs.k8s.io

```

> This is the official repo for Cluster API.

steps.title: Install the Cluster API Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
CRDs are necessary for Cluster API to function correctly as they define the custom resources used by the operator.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api.crds.yaml

```

> This installs the required CRDs for version 1.12.4.

steps.title: Install Cluster API controller
steps.description:
This step installs the Cluster API controller into your Kubernetes cluster, which manages the lifecycle of clusters.

```bash

helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version 1.12.4

```

> Ensure to specify the correct namespace and version.

steps.title: Create a Cluster API Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Cluster API cluster using a custom resource definition.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
    kind: MyInfrastructureCluster
    name: my-infra
EOF

```

> Replace 'MyInfrastructureCluster' with your actual infrastructure provider.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
    kind: MyInfrastructureCluster
    name: my-infra
EOF

steps.title: Verify Cluster API installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Cluster API components are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kube-system | grep cluster-api

```

> You should see the Cluster API controller pods running.
commands: kubectl get pods -n kube-system | grep cluster-api

uninstall.title: Uninstall Cluster API
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Cluster API components and CRDs from your cluster. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if clusters managed by CAPI are not deleted first.

```bash

helm uninstall cluster-api --namespace kube-system

kubectl delete -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api.crds.yaml

```

> This removes the Cluster API controller.
> This removes the CRDs.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Cluster API
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your cluster configurations. This step will upgrade the Cluster API to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -o yaml > cluster-backup.yaml

helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version 1.12.4

kubectl get pods -n kube-system | grep cluster-api

```

> Backup current cluster configurations.
> This upgrades the Cluster API to version 1.12.4.
> Verify that the Cluster API pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n kube-system | grep cluster-api

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "Cluster" in version "cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Cluster CRD is not installed or not available in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs using the command: kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api.crds.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to connect to the API server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually means that the Kubernetes API server is down or unreachable.

**Fix:**
Check the status of your Kubernetes cluster and ensure that the API server is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'unable to recognize "": no matches for kind "Cluster" in version "cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Cluster API controller is not running or not properly installed.

**Fix:**
Verify the installation of the Cluster API controller and check the logs for errors.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur due to network issues or the Kubernetes API server being overloaded.

**Fix:**
Check your network connectivity and the health of your Kubernetes cluster.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new infrastructure providers and improved error handling.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old API version 'v1alpha3' for clusters.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.12.3, ensure to update your cluster configurations to match the new API version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get clusters' showing your clusters with their statuses.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
    kind: MyInfrastructureCluster
    name: my-infra
EOF
kubectl get pods -n kube-system | grep cluster-api

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and the necessary tools installed.
